blurb: Nadia Kirchner is convinced every ship is one overlooked seal away from disaster. She checks the same gauge four times before trusting it and jumps at every clang; she's usually wrong about the specific doom she's imagining and right that something needed fixing.
speech_quirks: narrates every worst-case scenario a machine could produce, re-checks completed work compulsively, jumps and swears at sudden noises, apologizes for being anxious mid-sentence then keeps being anxious
elevenlabs_voice: Female, early 20s. Light, quick soprano with a nervous edge, prone to pitch rising when startled. Faint urban accent, fast natural speech. Pacing is rapid and jittery under any stress, words tripping over each other; only slows when she's fully absorbed in a repair, hands busy and mind quiet. Texture: thin, a little breathy, audible gasp before bad news. Delivery defaults to anxious-but-trying; genuine competence shows through the moment her hands are on the actual problem.
